title: Boat Rental in Fort Lauderdale: Costs, Choices & Tips for 2026
html:
Thinking about hiring a boat in Fort Lauderdale? The Venice of America offers easy access to the Intracoastal Waterway and the Atlantic Ocean, with skippered charters from around USD 350 for a half-day. A good place to start is the listings on BoatHire24, which feature everything from runabouts to luxury yachts.

Where to set off in Fort Lauderdale

Fort Lauderdale is laced with navigable waterways. Popular departure points include the Bahia Mar Yachting Center, a full-service marina right on the beachfront, and the Fort Lauderdale Marina on the New River. From there you can head east past Port Everglades out to the Atlantic, or cruise north along the Intracoastal Waterway to Pompano Beach and Hillsboro Inlet. To the south, the route passes through Hollywood and Dania Beach into Biscayne Bay. Many charters also offer pick-ups at the Lauderdale Yacht Club or the Harbour Towne Marina in Dania. You can skippered charters in Fort Lauderdale for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on your group size and the experience you want. The following list shows common options available for skippered charters in Fort Lauderdale. One option worth a look is the Fairline Targa 43 charter page.

  1. Centre Console Fishing Boat, best for offshore angling or a fast run to the reefs; carries up to 6 guests; from USD 450 for a half-day.
  2. Bowrider or Deck Boat, ideal for families exploring the canals and sandbars; seats up to 10; from USD 300 for half a day.
  3. Catamaran, stable and spacious for a full-day cruise; up to 12 guests; from USD 800 for a day.
  4. Motor Yacht (e.g. Azimut or Sunseeker), luxury cruising for corporate or special events; capacity 12 guests; from USD 1,500 for a half-day.
  5. Fairline Targa 43, a sporty express cruiser perfect for a sunset run; sleeps 2, 4; see the Fairline Targa 43 charter page for current pricing.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale?

Prices range from around USD 50 per person for a shared tour to over USD 2,000 for a crewed yacht. Here is a typical price band breakdown:

  • Shared group tour (2, 3 hours): USD 50, 100 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, private): USD 300, 600.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, skipper included): USD 800, 1,500.
  • Crewed yacht (motor or sail, full-day with crew): USD 1,500, 3,500.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale?

In Florida you generally need a Boating Safety Education ID Card if you were born on or after 1 January 1988, though skippered charters provide the captain. Most bareboat rentals will require the card or a temporary certificate. Renters who book a skipper need no licence. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. If you own a boat, you can become a host and earn from spare days.

"The Intracoastal Waterway can get busy on weekends. Aim to depart before 10 am to enjoy the calmest sea conditions and avoid the queue at Port Everglades."

Best season for boat rental in Fort Lauderdale

The peak season runs from December to April, when temperatures are mild and the sea breeze, often a southeasterly, keeps things comfortable. Summer (May, October) is hotter and more humid, with afternoon thunderstorms. However, rates can be lower outside the winter peak. BoatHire24 offers bookings throughout the year.

At a glance

  • Price band: USD 50, 3,500 per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: 4 or 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 12 on most private charters
  • Currency: US Dollar

Questions about boat rental in Fort Lauderdale

Can you rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale without a captain?

Yes, many rental companies offer self-drive boats for those with a valid boating licence, but a skipper is recommended for navigating the Intracoastal and inlets.

What is the best time of day for a boat trip in Fort Lauderdale?

For sightseeing, the midday sun offers clear views; for a leisurely cruise, the late afternoon sunset slot is popular as the sea breeze drops.

How far can you get in a half-day charter from Fort Lauderdale?

A half-day cruising north can take you up to Hillsboro Inlet, about 12 nautical miles, or south to Haulover Sandbar, roughly 10 nautical miles away.

Are there any extra fees when renting a boat in Fort Lauderdale?

Always check for additional costs such as fuel, gratuity for the crew, and docking fees at certain marinas; these are usually itemised on the booking confirmation.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale or list your own. If you have a boat to share, consider turning it into income by choosing to become a host.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fort Lauderdale and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
